title: croton prostratus
intro: Croton prostratus, also known as the creeping croton, is a low-growing, spreading plant native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia, characterized by its small, rounded leaves with yellow, orange, or red veins. The medicinal properties of Croton prostratus are primarily attributed to its flavonoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce pain, improve digestion, and lower blood pressure. In traditional herbal medicine, Croton prostratus is prepared as a decoction or infusion, with various herbal preparations involving the leaves, roots, and stems being used to treat a range of ailments.
